text: When designing a REST API, following best practices makes your service easy to use and maintain. First, clear and consistent endpoints are essential. For example, use plural nouns like `/users` instead of confusing names, and keep them focused on resources. Each endpoint should return appropriate status codes, such as `200` for success or `404` if a resource is not found. Versioning is also important; by including a version number in the URL, like `/v1/users`, you can make updates without breaking existing users. Finally, good documentation is a must. It helps developers understand how to use your API, what to expect in responses, and how errors are handled.
